title: Why does buildah pull print info output to stderr?
body:
**Description**

`buildah pull` prints output to stderr which should actually be in stdout

I just got this bug report in ansible-bender: https://github.com/ansible-community/ansible-bender/issues/193

This behaviour makes it hard to integrate buildah into a project since stderr contains text which is not composed of errors and hence makes it confusing to users.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

```
$ buildah pull alpine >./stdout 2>./stderr

$ cat stdout
965ea09ff2ebd2b9eeec88cd822ce156f6674c7e99be082c7efac3c62f3ff652

$ cat stderr
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob sha256:89d9c30c1d48bac627e5c6cb0d1ed1eec28e7dbdfbcc04712e4c79c0f83faf17
Copying config sha256:965ea09ff2ebd2b9eeec88cd822ce156f6674c7e99be082c7efac3c62f3ff652
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
```

**Describe the results you expected:**

```
$ buildah pull alpine >./stdout 2>./stderr

$ cat stdout
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob sha256:89d9c30c1d48bac627e5c6cb0d1ed1eec28e7dbdfbcc04712e4c79c0f83faf17
Copying config sha256:965ea09ff2ebd2b9eeec88cd822ce156f6674c7e99be082c7efac3c62f3ff652
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
965ea09ff2ebd2b9eeec88cd822ce156f6674c7e99be082c7efac3c62f3ff652

$ cat stderr  # read as "nothing"
```


**Output of `rpm -q buildah` or `apt list buildah`:**

```
buildah-1.11.4-2.fc30.x86_64
```

comments.body: I believe this is following the Docker behavior.  allowing users to just grab the image id from stdout and all of the progress information goes to stderr.

Got it, thank you. We were able to figure a nice fix this on our end, so we can close:

https://github.com/ansible-community/ansible-bender/issues/193#issuecomment-555528054
